candidates.stmt:
Cathy has lived locally for 35 years and served as an Aveley
councillor for two. She is known for being responsive and proactive, holding advice surgeries and organising litter picks.
Cathy works on local issues across the ward and is committed to protecting Aveley’s community feel while tackling the day-to-day problems affecting people’s lives, and doing all she can to protect our green spaces.

Srikanth has served as an Aveley councillor for four years, improving
roads, fixing potholes, resurfacing, and maintaining local parks.
He has helped residents with housing and SEND issues, tackled an-
social behaviour, and serves as a Neighbourhood Watch
Coordinator with Essex Police. He is campaigning for a cleaner
Aveley, including installing new lier bins across the ward.

Amin is a dedicated local campaigner who has lived and worked
in Thurrock for many years. He has organised community
campaigns and worked with residents on local issues.
Amin is passionate about safer streets, protecng green spaces,
improving local services and supporng families through the
cost-of-living crisis, and will be a strong, local voice.

I would like to make sure that residents of Aveley are the ones shaping their local area, giving power to the people in deciding what happens to their village. For too long we have been asked for our opinion during consultations, just to be ignored as private investors are prioritised. I have lived in Aveley for 22 years, and would stand for many issues I have noticed myself such as:

- Fixing potholes caused by the neglect of our roads
- Preventing the illegal use of our roads by HGV
- Preventing fly-tipping in known locations such as Ship Lane
- Clearance of public footpath 145 which has been massively overgrown and therefore illegally inaccessible for years
- Protection and further accessibility of our green spaces
- Activities for young people such as bringing back the youth club and further investment into the library
- More public bins to prevent rubbish on the streets
- Blocking construction of private luxury housing that no one in the area can actually afford, and instead building much needed affordable council housing
- Work with other councils across Thurrock to bring bus prices back to their previous prices, before the removal of the national £2 fare cap
- Standing against proposed data centre development near Aveley, such as the ones proposed for North Ockendon, and the former Arena Essex site. Both of which would pollute the area, destroy green belt land, and raise water and energy costs.

These are only a few of my own personal thoughts on what the area needs, but as stated before I would always prioritise and amplify the voice of my community. I am a working class lifelong resident of Aveley and as such would stand for the needs of Aveley. If elected my first action would be to speak with as many residents as possible, and prioritise the most pressing issues we as a community face.

Vikki has served as a councillor for Chafford
Hundred for three years and is a long-term
local resident whose children went to school
in the area.
Vikki is known for being responsive and
hardworking, representing residents’ concerns,
supporting local services and helping keep
Chafford Hundred a great place to live.

Julie is a registered nurse with over 30 years
of experience and has lived in the UK since
1990.
Julie has been active in local politics and
community campaigning and is passionate
about public service, community wellbeing
and listening to residents. She is committed
to serving the community with compassion
and dedication.

**Vote Green on 7th May: Real Hope, Real Change for Thurrock**

This election is about more than just a cross on a ballot paper. It’s about choosing a future where our community and our planet come first. The Green Party offers a bold, practical alternative to the status quo, focusing on the issues that matter most to our daily lives.

**Why Vote Green?**
A Fairer Housing Market: We believe everyone deserves a safe, secure, and affordable place to call home. Our "Fairer, Greener Homes Guarantee" pushes for 150,000 new social homes a year, local rent controls to keep prices fair, and an end to no-fault evictions.

**Restoring Our Local Environment**: From protecting the Green Belt to tackling the sewage crisis, Greens are the only ones committed to a Rights of Nature Act. We advocate for taking water companies back into public ownership to ensure our rivers and coastlines are clean and protected for future generations.

**Funding Our Vital Services**: Years of underfunding have left local councils struggling. The Green Party is calling for a £5 billion annual increase in local government funding to secure the future of our social care, youth services, and community spaces. 
 
**Action on the Climate Emergency**: We don't just talk about the climate; we act. By investing in the transition to a zero-carbon economy at the local level, we can create green jobs, lower energy bills through home insulation, and improve public transport for everyone.

**A New Kind of Politics**: Green councillors aren't whipped by party bosses in Westminster. They are free to speak up for their residents and work collaboratively to find real solutions for Thurrock.

On 7th May, vote for a party that puts people and the planet at the heart of every decision. **Vote Green.**

I want to make sure that resident’s voices are heard and that local people are the ones shaping their
area.
I’m keen to support people practically, as in my day job I am a mental health therapist and often it’s
the situational factors (eg. housing, finances, work etc…) that have the biggest impact on people’s
wellbeing.

Hi All, i just wanted to introduce myself.

I have just been on 'The Belmont'
delivering my election address for the local elections on the 7th May.

I really was a local lad.
My family home was a mid terrace 2 bed house in Belmont Road.

I went to Grays comprehensive school.

My friends & i used to hang out over Parker Road park. Playing 'footie' most of the time...or runouts down the pit that has been transformed into Badgers Dene.

We used to hang around down Church path, by the Wharf. 
At the opposite end is St Peter's & St Paul's Church where my parents married.

We used to go to Blockbusters in Grays High Street or the 'offie' along London Rd by Castle Rd & go round each other's houses to watch whatever video we rented out.

My local was The Wharf pub.
Had some really good times down there.
Oh the memories! 🙄 🙃 😊

Had friends down Seabrooke Rise, would go round their homes after school sometimes.

I am Grays through & through.
Grays is my home.
My family were Grays through & through too.
Just a normal guy (ok...I have an interest in politics (& the Solar System)) so normal...ish.

I am a wood machinist by trade. I'm at my happiest when using my hands & not sitting behind a desk.

Anyway, i would just like to say i would be very proud to represent the area/ward of Grays Riverside where my heart lies & where I know best.

Thanks
Mark

Aaron has served as a councillor since 2023
and is active in the community, helping at
the local hub and pantry and supporting
community events.
Aaron works for Parcelforce and is a union
representative, he is committed to
supporting residents, being accessible
and continuing to serve the community.

Lee has lived in Thurrock for over 20 years and
raised his family locally. She has served as a
councillor for Purfleet-on-Thames for five
years, helping residents with housing,
anti-social behaviour, council tax and local
issues.
Lee supports the Purfleet Pantry, Heritage
Centre and Purfleet Forum, and campaigns
for regeneration and action on fly-tipping.

Cornell Olive HEISER is standing for local councillor in Purfleet-on-Thames because our community deserves strong, experienced, and committed representation.

Cornell has 22 years of experience working within housing, 17 + years as a staff council representative, and hands‑on experience working directly within the community. Cornell spent her career helping people secure safe, stable homes, supporting families through real challenges, helping people sustain their tenancies and being a voice for those who has no voice.

These roles have shaped her passion for strengthening our community, improving local services, and ensuring every resident is heard.

If elected, she will bring practical experience, accountability, and a genuine commitment to our Purfleet-on-Thames’ future!

Councillor Qaisar Abbas is fully supporting Cornell Olive Heiser and her colleague Angela BOLESWORTH in their campaign.

Cornell and Angela are standing for local councillors in Purfleet-on-Thames ward because our community deserves strong, experienced, and committed representation.

Both candidates and Cllr Abbas are requesting you to please VOTE for them on 7th May. The Polls will open 7am - 10pm.

Neil is an independent councillor on Thurrock Council, an award-winning editor and investigative journalist, a stalwart supporter of the south Essex community in general and a keen activist and campaigner on behalf of a varied array of local organisations and charities.

He has lived and raised a family in Stanford-le-Hope, where he has lived for more than 20 years.

Neil says: "I was honoured and privileged to be elected to represent the Stanford West ward last year (Check out my ward councillor's page here) and during the past 12 months it has been immensely rewarding to help a significant number of local residents on personal issues, to help improve local facilities and services and to challenge the failings of the incumbent administration of Thurrock Council.

"There have been many high points in the past year and moments I will treasure when I have been able to make a difference to the lives of local people.

"At the same time there have been equally disappointing moments when bureaucracy, cold-heartedness or the lack of money caused by the council’s catastrophic financial failure have seen case work end in failure, residents left anguished and distressed and significant failures in the service offered by the council.

"However, the positives generally outweigh the negative and I have been honoured to win the nomination of council colleagues to play an increased role in the next civic year by taking the responsibility of chairing the overview and scrutiny committee looking at many services offered to residents involving health and housing among other things. I will be working hard to continue to make a positive difference and to serve on a number of other committees.

"In addition, I have been asked to take the role of spokesperson for seven fellow independent councillors on the council and we look forward to growing that number in the 2025 local elections. I have noted the return of six independent councillors in Basildon and I know they will be working hard w

Hi, thanks for taking the time to find out who’s standing in your local area. I'm standing for election because I care about this community. I believe the Green Party's values and ambitions match with what I think Thurrock needs. Every one of us deserves to be heard and be proud of where we live.

I was originally born and brought up in east London and have lived in Thurrock for over ten years. I’m an NHS professional, mum of two young kids, wife, and owner of a cat (or maybe the cat owns me…).

I’m aware of ongoing concerns such as sub-standard council housing and a lack of social housing; rising crime and antisocial behaviour along with people feeling that the police aren’t responsive; dissatisfaction with local NHS services; an increase in fly tipping and rubbish including dog fouling; the train station not being fit for purpose. At times it feels as if we as a community are divided and not always listened to.

Nationally, the Green Party have called for a £5 billion yearly increase in local government funding which would help towards the underfunding and mismanagement of public money that we as residents are experiencing. They have also called for local authorities to invest in youth services, community-based policing, and protecting green spaces from development. We need more housing but we should also protect green spaces like Thameside and Langdon Nature Reserves that we are lucky to have.

A vote for me would be a vote for Thurrock to be a part of positive change, hope, and unity for all of us.

Qaisar Abbas is Conservative Councillor for West Thurrock and South Stifford Ward with a great track record of delivering projects. He is standing again in this election along-with Elena Pelin. Both live in the West Thurrock and South Stifford Ward and understand the local issues. With Qaisar’s experience and Elena’s fresh perspective, they will work hard to improve Local Services. Qaisar and Elena will always be ready to listen, respond and act on what matter most to you and ensure that improvements benefit everyone in this area and no one is left behind.

Qaisar Abbas' previous work as a local councillor, and Qaisar and Elena's leaflets can be viewed at www.facebook.com/qaisarabbasuk

Both candidates are requesting residents of West Thurrock and South Stifford to VOTE for them on 7th May 2026. The Polls will open from 7am till 10pm and voters need Photo ID to cast their vote.

Elena PELIN and Qaisar ABBAS are Conservative Councillor Candidates for West Thurrock and South Stifford Ward. Qaisar is your local Councillor with a great track record of delivering projects. With Qaisar’s experience and Elena’s fresh perspective, they will work hard to improve Local Services. Qaisar and Elena will always be ready to listen, respond and act on what matter most to you and ensure that improvements benefit everyone in this area and no one is left behind. This is Local Election and both live locally in West Thurrock and South Stifford Ward.

Elena and Qaisar's leaflets can be viewed at www.facebook.com/qaisarabbasuk

Both candidates are requesting residents of West Thurrock and South Stifford to VOTE for them on 7th May 2026. The Polls will open from 7am till 10pm and voters need Photo ID to cast their vote.

Shalini has lived locally since 2020 and
previously worked in the Post Office supporting
residents. She helps organise community
events including Holi, Diwali, summer fairs and
business networking events.
Shalini is passionate about cleaner streets,
safer neighbourhoods and improving local
services while bringing the community
together

RON GREEN
Ron has over 15 years of management
experience leading teams and solving
problems. He is committed to being a visible
and approachable councillor, supporting local
services and working with community groups.
Ron wants to represent residents and make a
positive difference in the local community.
